sect1.title: Introduction
sect1.para: The ExpMC20 class, implemented in Cython, is a specialized event template within the AnalysisG framework, designed to handle data specific to experimental Monte Carlo 20 (MC20) events. It inherits from the generic EventTemplate class, extending its functionalities to incorporate features and structures relevant to this particular dataset. This class provides a high-performance interface to an underlying C++ event object tailored for experimental MC20 analysis.

sect1.title: The ExpMC20 Class
sect1.para: ExpMC20 is a cdef class, indicating its implementation as a Cython extension type. It inherits from EventTemplate, thereby gaining all the base functionalities for event indexing, weighting, and tree/branch management. Its primary role is to encapsulate a C++ exp_mc20 object, which likely contains specific data members and methods pertinent to experimental MC20 events.

listitem.para: __cinit__(self): The Cython "constructor" equivalent, called during object allocation. It initializes a new C++ exp_mc20 object and assigns its pointer to self.exp. Crucially, it then upcasts self.exp to an event_template* and assigns it to self.ptr, allowing ExpMC20 to be treated as a generic EventTemplate while still managing its specialized C++ object.
__init__(self): The standard Python initializer. This method is intentionally left empty, as initialization logic is handled by __cinit__ and the underlying C++ object.

__dealloc__(self): The Cython "destructor" equivalent, called upon object deallocation. It deallocates the C++ exp_mc20 object pointed to by self.exp (via self.ptr), ensuring proper memory management for the specialized event data.

sect1.title: C++ Interaction
sect1.para:
The ExpMC20 class leverages Cython's capabilities for seamless C++ integration:
cdef class ExpMC20: Declares ExpMC20 as a Cython extension type.
cdef exp_mc20 *exp: A C++ pointer to an exp_mc20 object, which is the specialized C++ representation of an experimental MC20 event.
self.ptr = <event_template*>(self.exp): This line demonstrates C++ upcasting, where a pointer to a derived class (exp_mc20) is assigned to a pointer to its base class (event_template). This allows ExpMC20 to fulfill the EventTemplate interface while internally managing its specific exp_mc20 data.
from AnalysisG.core.event_template cimport EventTemplate: Imports the base EventTemplate class, from which ExpMC20 inherits.
